title: Why was it Removed 10 Question
wikitext:
[]: Why was it Removed? (1.0 Question)             Quote Post by DAVEvangelion » Thu Dec 03, 2009 7:27 pm In the original series when Shinji runs away the first time he is walking through a field and comes across Kensuke playing army by himself (Ep 4). They proceed to have a long conversation in which Kensuke reveals that the two of them are similar because they have both lost their mother. However, in Rebuild 1.0 this scene was removed. Shinji decides to return to NERV and turns around to find the soldiers ready to retrieve him. Why was the scene with Shinji and Kensuke removed? It was one of the few times that Kensuke's character saw any kind of development through a backstory.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Dec 03, 2009 7:33 pm Maybe it was for runtime reasons and/or the writers felt that it wasn't really necessary to have.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Thu Dec 03, 2009 7:35 pm One phrase......"time constraints" If Rebuild was a tv show, there'd probably be no problem in having that scene be re-animated and included. But in the original NGE, it took up i think 4-5 min of time.... Way too much if you ask me for character development of a supporting character you dont see too much of. Meh...it was also staff and Anno's choice for not extending running time for 1.0.

[]:             Quote Post by Heromaster111 » Thu Dec 03, 2009 8:15 pm In all fairness Ep.4 is pretty much trimmed to like 5 mins in Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Dec 03, 2009 8:45 pm Just my own little lame theory I always kinda felt that the sequence where Shinji spends the night in that tent talking with Kensuke may have been intended as a sort of bookend scene tied to episode twenty-four when Shinji spends the night at Kaworu's apartment talking about everything that's happened to him up to that point. Perhaps their decision to remove it for Rebuild means that they plan on Kaworu playing a somewhat different role than he did in the original series, which seems like a possibility since his character is introduced into the storyline far earlier (not unlike the manga). Also, what everyone else said regarding time constraints. If you ask me, it's a shame, because I always liked Kensuke and would have enjoyed seeing his character receive some more screen time.

[]: Re: Why was it Removed? (1.0 Question)             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Dec 03, 2009 10:18 pm DAVEvangelion wrote: Why was the scene with Shinji and Kensuke removed? It was one of the few times that Kensuke's character saw any kind of development through a backstory.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Thu Dec 03, 2009 10:30 pm every time I see the men in black in that scene in Rebuild, I always quip "Mr. Ikari, its been a long time" in my best Agent Smith vocal impersonation

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Thu Dec 03, 2009 10:43 pm Here come the Men In Black~~~~~~~~~ And all that you remember~~~~~~~~ That's what I think of when I think of men in black or section 2.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Thu Dec 03, 2009 10:45 pm yeah I also wonder if the agents from "The Matrix" worked with Jay & Kay at one point but anyways...on topic, although that scene was fun (Far more so in the manga. Hell, manga Asuka is funny as hell, as I read vol. 4 last night back to back with vol. 3), it was perhaps removed due to time constraints, as well as the other reasons our peers posted

[]: Re: Why was it Removed? (1.0 Question)             Quote Post by DAVEvangelion » Thu Dec 03, 2009 10:59 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Probably because it was never part of the story to begin with.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Dec 03, 2009 11:30 pm BC Baron wrote: I always kinda felt that the sequence where Shinji spends the night in that tent talking with Kensuke may have been intended as a sort of bookend scene tied to episode twenty-four when Shinji spends the night at Kaworu's apartment talking about everything that's happened to him up to that point.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Sun Dec 06, 2009 12:32 am NAveryW wrote: Episode 24 wasn't anywhere near planned out when that episode was written. Take a look at the synopses of the rough drafts of episode 24 and note how much it changed even after it got close to production.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Mon Dec 07, 2009 11:59 am The entire escape arc should just have been cut out of 1.0. The only thing it did was segmenting the story, which added a lot to the Death-ish feel of the movie. Instead, they should have found a way to properly introduce Rei earlier.

[]:             Quote Post by Kamina » Mon Dec 07, 2009 2:13 pm They probably removed it because it would of made the film another 5 min longer or so. They will probably get rid of this complex of an explanation of how the EVA function and how pilots are selected in order to to simplify and stream line the story. That or they will introduce the concept later in a less time consuming manner if they keep the motherly soul in the EVA idea around.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Dec 07, 2009 3:10 pm BC Baron wrote: Okay, take it easy; please remain calm.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Dec 10, 2009 4:18 am NAveryW wrote: What did I say to possibly insinuate I wasn't calm?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 10, 2009 10:56 am Er, Naveryw is a pretty easy-going guy so I'm not sure where the "remain as such" is coming from. Anyway, it's nice how even with little things like no Kensuke scene (not just doing Operation Yashima right), 1.0 is more in-tune with how Anno wanted things for the series given their time and budget with the films.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Dec 10, 2009 11:25 am DAVEvangelion wrote: In the original series when Shinji runs away the first time he is walking through a field and comes across Kensuke playing army by himself (Ep 4). They proceed to have a long conversation in which Kensuke reveals that the two of them are similar because they have both lost their mother. However, in Rebuild 1.0 this scene was removed. Shinji decides to return to NERV and turns around to find the soldiers ready to retrieve him. Why was the scene with Shinji and Kensuke removed? It was one of the few times that Kensuke's character saw any kind of development through a backstory.
